'''Ubasameir''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with an inborn tenderness for [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]].<ref name="RM" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="GCW">[[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']] establishes that [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] were in active conflict with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a [[Time/Legends|time]] period identified by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' as the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].</ref> some Ubasameir worked in the [[Sub-Adult Center]] on the [[Bazaar Deck]] of the [[Kuari Princess/Legends|''Kuari Princess'']],<ref name="RM" /> a giant [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liner]].<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|III|234|''Kuari Princess''}}</ref> [[Parent/Legends|Parents]] were able to drop off their younglings to the center for the Ubasameir and [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to provide educational and recreational care. They were present on the cruise [[Starship/Legends|ship]] when it was [[Attack on the Kuari Princess|attacked]] by [[Riders of the Maelstrom|pirates]] when passing through [[Maelstrom Nebula/Legends|the Maelstrom]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]].<ref name="RM" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ubasameir first appeared in [[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']], a [[1989]] [[roleplaying game]] book written by [[Ray Winninger]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="RM" />

==Appearances==
*[[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']] {{Reprint|Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)}} {{1st}}
